layers.title: Tracker And Config
layers.summary: The first layer adds the request-driven success-rate tracker and the dynamic gflags that control it. The tracker settles fixed windows, gates on minimum samples, suppresses duplicate switches, and backs off failed switch attempts.

layers.title: Failure Signal Wiring
layers.summary: On top of the tracker, the client worker API records URMA write outcomes and Get response fallback outcomes. The common remote API invokes a callback only when a settled window is unhealthy and the tracker has claimed the switch gate.

layers.title: Asynchronous Worker Switch
layers.summary: The final runtime layer connects unhealthy URMA windows to the existing asynchronous worker-switch machinery. The new switch reason keeps the current worker available during data-plane failover, preserving foreground TCP fallback availability if no better candidate is available.

layers.title: Tracker Unit Coverage
layers.summary: The unit layer validates the tracker state machine independently of client traffic. It covers full-window settlement, sample floors, strict threshold behavior, disable-and-clear semantics, backoff progression, healthy-window reset, and concurrent boundary settlement.
layers.diff:
+ds_cc_test(
+    name = "urma_success_rate_tracker_test",
+    srcs = ["urma_success_rate_tracker_test.cpp"],
+    deps = [
+        "//src/datasystem/client:urma_success_rate_tracker",
+        "//src/datasystem/common/flags:ds_flags",
+        "//src/datasystem/common/log:common_log",
+        "//tests/ut:ut_common",
+    ],
+)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, DoesNotTriggerBeforeFullWindow)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, RequiresMinimumSampleCount)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, UsesStrictThreshold)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, ThresholdZeroDisablesAndClearsWindow)
+TEST_F(UrmaSuccessRateTrackerTest, ConcurrentBoundaryTriggersOnlyOnce)

layers.title: Client Failover ST
layers.summary: The system-test layer exercises the same logic through real KV client traffic, service discovery, injected URMA failures, and worker restarts. It validates Set and Get failure sampling, TCP fallback continuity, switch failure behavior, dynamic config updates, backoff reset, and local-worker recovery.
layers.diff:
+ds_cc_test(
+    name = "kv_client_urma_failover_test",
+    srcs = ["kv_client_urma_failover_test.cpp"],
+    tags = ["manual"],
+    deps = KV_COMMON_DEPS + [
+        "//src/datasystem/client:urma_success_rate_tracker",
+        "//src/datasystem/client:service_discovery",
+        "//src/datasystem/common/flags:ds_flags",
+    ],
+)
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LEVEL1_RemoteUbFallbackSwitchByDiscovery)
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LEVEL1_RemoteGetTcpFallbackSwitchByDiscovery)
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LEVEL1_RemoteSwitchFailureKeepsCurrentWorkerAvailable)
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LEVEL1_DynamicThresholdUpdateAppliesAtWindowSettlement)
+TEST_F(KVClientUrmaFailoverTest, LEVEL1_LocalFailRemoteFailThenSwitchBack)

layers.title: Development Provenance
layers.summary: This layer records the conversational provenance for the requested URMA data-plane failover work. It is not runtime logic, but it documents the task prompt, implementation intent, and local validation transcript included with the PR.
layers.diff:
+{"type": "user", "uuid": "u1", "parentUuid": null, "timestamp": "2026-06-11T09:00:37.000Z", "message": {"role": "user", "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "We keep losing latency when a client's URMA (RDMA) data plane goes bad but the worker is still alive — the client stays pinned to the dead fast-path. Implement client-side failover: when the URMA data plane is persistently failing, switch the client to another worker. Add a UrmaSuccessRateTracker, two gflags (success-rate ratio + min sample count), wire it into the client worker API, and add a UT + ST."}]}}
+{"type": "assistant", "uuid": "a14", "parentUuid": "u13", "timestamp": "2026-06-11T09:08:38.000Z", "message": {"id": "msg_14", "role": "assistant", "model": "claude-opus-4-8", "usage": {"input_tokens": 3000, "output_tokens": 90, "cache_creation_input_tokens": 0, "cache_read_input_tokens": 18000}, "content": [{"type": "text", "text": "Done. Added UrmaSuccessRateTracker with a 0.5 success-rate threshold and a 5-sample minimum, two gflags, wired it into the client worker API, and added the UT. Tests pass."}]}}

layers.title: Client Flag Guide
layers.summary: The operator-facing docs add the two new client runtime parameters after the provenance layer records why the feature exists. The guide describes hot-update support, default values, valid ranges, and the disable semantics for a zero success-rate threshold.
layers.diff:
+## 客户端运行参数
+
+以下 gflag 参数可通过 `DATASYSTEM_CLIENT_CONFIG_PATH` 配置文件设置，支持热更新。
+
+| 序号 | 名称 | 默认值 | 取值范围 | 含义/用途 |
+|------|------|--------|----------|-----------|
+| 29 | `urma_failover_success_rate_ratio` | `0.5` | `[0.0, 1.0]` | Client 侧 URMA 数据面成功率阈值。一个完整统计窗口的成功率严格低于该 ratio 时，client 尝试切换 worker；设置为 `0.0` 表示关闭 URMA 故障切换功能，同时清空当前窗口状态。 |
+| 30 | `urma_failover_min_sample_count` | `5` | `> 0` | 每个 `client_dead_timeout_s` 统计窗口内进行故障切换判断前所需的最小 URMA 数据面样本数。样本不足时即使成功率很低也不会触发切换，避免少量请求误触发。 |
+4.客户端运行参数通过 `DATASYSTEM_CLIENT_CONFIG_PATH` 配置文件设置。

summary: This PR adds client-side URMA data-plane health tracking and failover so persistently unhealthy fast-path traffic can switch workers while preserving TCP fallback availability.
